intro:
There is no information about a plant called Leptopharynx leptoglossa. However, I can provide information about a plant called Leptandra or Leptandra virginica which is sometimes referred to as "American Fenwort".

Leptandra is a plant species that belongs to the family Menyanthaceae. It is a perennial herb native to North America, commonly found in wetlands and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation, and its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. 

The bioactive constituents of Leptandra include flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Leptandra include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions.
